title: Valuing American options and Flexible Forwards contracts in time-dependent models
abstract: arXiv:2606.27335v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: A flexible forward (FF) is a customized FX hedging instrument that guarantees a fixed exchange rate while letting the holder choose the delivery date within a pre-agreed window. It is therefore an American-style option on timing, and its valuation must respect the volatility skew of the underlying currency pair. We price FF contracts (and, more generally, American options) under a time-inhomogeneous Heston model which captures the forward-skew term structure while preserving analytical tractability through a recursive (matrix) Riccati solution for the joint characteristic function. Extending the integral-equation (decomposition) approach to time-dependent coefficients, we derive a Volterra equation characterizing the early-exercise surface. The expectation in the decomposition formula is evaluated by two complementary spectral methods: a double cosine (COS) expansion of the transition density, and a damped-Sinc (DSINC) local-basis scheme

Title: Valuing American options and Flexible Forwards contracts in time-dependent models
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27335v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: A flexible forward (FF) is a customized FX hedging instrument that guarantees a fixed exchange rate while letting the holder choose the delivery date within a pre-agreed window. It is therefore an American-style option on timing, and its valuation must respect the volatility skew of the underlying currency pair. We price FF contracts (and, more generally, American options) under a time-inhomogeneous Heston model which captures the forward-skew term structure while preserving analytical tractability through a recursive (matrix) Riccati solution for the joint characteristic function. Extending the integral-equation (decomposition) approach to time-dependent coefficients, we derive a Volterra equation characterizing the early-exercise surface. The expectation in the decomposition formula is evaluated by two complementary spectral methods: a double cosine (COS) expansion of the transition density, and a damped-Sinc (DSINC) local-basis scheme
translated_title: 时间依赖模型中的美式期权与灵活远期合约定价
translated_abstract: 灵活远期合约是一种定制化的外汇对冲工具，它保证固定汇率的同时允许持有人在预先约定的窗口期内选择交割日期。因此，这是一种关于时机选择的美式期权，其估值必须考虑基础货币对的波动率偏斜。我们在时间非齐次Heston模型下对灵活远期合约（以及更一般的美式期权）进行定价，该模型能够捕捉远期偏斜的期限结构，同时通过联合特征函数的递归（矩阵）Riccati解保持解析可处理性。将积分方程（分解）方法扩展到时间依赖系数，我们推导出刻画提前执行边界的Volterra方程。分解公式中的期望通过两种互补的谱方法评估：过渡密度的双余弦（COS）展开，以及阻尼Sinc（DSINC）局部基方案。
tldr: 本文提出在时间非齐次Heston模型下，通过递归矩阵Riccati解和Volterra方程，结合双余弦展开与阻尼Sinc两种谱方法，为灵活远期合约（美式期权）提供高效定价框架。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes a pricing framework for flexible forward contracts (American options) under time-inhomogeneous Heston model, using recursive matrix Riccati solutions and Volterra equations, combined with double cosine expansion and damped-Sinc spectral methods.
